hero.title: 6 Days Best of Bali
hero.intro: Six private days exploring Bali without compromise — the iconic landmarks plus the ones most visitors never reach: the Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang, Tirta Gangga's royal water palace, and the hidden cascade of Kanto Lampo Waterfall.

breadcrumb.label: 6 Days Best of Bali Private Escape

hero.heroImageAlt: Lempuyang Temple Gates of Heaven Bali reflection Mount Agung

Full private vehicle for Day 5 (up to 10 hours)

overview.heading: Six Days to Experience the Full Range of Bali
body: Six days is the length of stay that finally lets Bali breathe. The first three days cover the island's most celebrated circuit — Uluwatu's clifftop, Ubud's highlands, the Floating Temple at Bedugul, and Tanah Lot at dusk — at a pace that allows each place to make its proper impression rather than a checkbox. Day four adds the sites that separate this package from every other: the Lempuyang Temple and its famous Gates of Heaven, a doorway that frames a perfect reflection of Mount Agung; the Tirta Gangga royal water palace with its ornate fountains and reflection pools; and Kanto Lampo Waterfall, a tiered cascade in the island's east that most Bali visitors never find.

Day five is entirely free — a private vehicle and driver for up to ten hours, to use however you want. Sleep in, find a beach club, explore a village, or revisit somewhere you wanted more time at. Day six is a gentle departure. This is Bali done properly — not rushed, not crowded, not shared with strangers.

items.title: Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang Temple
items.body: Pura Lempuyang Luhur sits at 1,175 metres on the slopes of Mount Lempuyang in east Bali. Its split gate — the Candi Bentar — frames a perfect symmetrical view of Mount Agung when the sky is clear, and when shot at the reflection pool below, the image is one of the most replicated photographs in Southeast Asia. The temple complex is genuinely sacred and the setting — mist, jungle, and a peak that has been worshipped since the 10th century — is humbling regardless of whether you have a camera.
items.imageAlt: Lempuyang Temple Gates of Heaven Bali reflection Mount Agung

items.title: Tirta Gangga Royal Water Palace
items.body: Built in 1948 by the last raja of Karangasem, Tirta Gangga is a complex of ornate pools, fountains, stepping stones, and water gardens laid out across terraced rice fields in east Bali. Koi-filled reflection ponds, stone statues, and lush tropical gardens surround the central spring, which is still considered holy by local Balinese. It is a place of extraordinary tranquillity — and architecturally, one of Bali's most complete traditional royal estates.
items.imageAlt: Tirta Gangga water palace ornate pools fountains east Bali

items.title: Kanto Lampo Waterfall — East Bali's Hidden Cascade
items.body: Kanto Lampo is a tiered waterfall east of Gianyar where water cascades over a broad face of black volcanic rock in multiple thin streams, creating a curtain effect unique among Bali's many waterfalls. A short walk through rice fields leads to the cascade, where it is possible to stand on the lower rocks and feel the spray. It appears on few standard Bali itineraries — which makes it one of the best stops on this one.
items.imageAlt: Kanto Lampo Waterfall east Bali volcanic rock curtain waterfall

items.title: Tanah Lot & Bedugul Floating Temple
items.body: Day three pairs two of Bali's most iconic landmarks. Pura Ulun Danu Beratan appears to float on the misty surface of highland Lake Beratan — a 17th-century temple that is one of Bali's most-photographed sites. The day ends at Tanah Lot, the 16th-century sea temple on a rocky islet that frames the definitive Bali sunset silhouette. Junglegold Chocolate Factory and Bloom Garden make the highland drive between them memorable.
items.imageAlt: Tanah Lot sea temple Bali sunset rocky islet ocean

items.title: Full Day with Private Vehicle — Use It How You Want
items.body: Day five is yours. The private driver and vehicle are included for up to ten hours — no fixed schedule, no guide agenda, just Bali and your preferences. Popular choices include Seminyak beach clubs, additional waterfalls, Bali Safari, Nusa Dua, rice terrace walks, or spa treatments. Meals are at your own expense.
items.imageAlt: Bali private vehicle driver highland road scenic

days.title: Arrive Bali — Melasti Beach, Uluwatu & Jimbaran Bay
days.description: Your English-speaking driver-guide meets you at Ngurah Rai Airport. The afternoon moves to Bali's southern peninsula — Melasti Beach first, a striking white-sand cove between limestone cliffs, then up to Uluwatu for the clifftop exploration and sweeping ocean views. The optional Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (RM35) is highly recommended. The evening ends with grilled BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay, tables on the sand, lanterns lit, waves rolling in nearby.
suggestedActivities: Optional Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (RM35)
BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay

days.title: Ubud Highlands, Rice Terraces, Mount Batur & Tirta Empul
days.description: A full day through Bali's cultural heartland. Celuk village's gold and silver jewellery workshops start the morning, followed by Ubud Royal Palace and the vibrant Ubud Traditional Market. Tegalalang's terraced rice fields — UNESCO-listed subak irrigation at its most scenic — offer a stop for photos, with an optional Bali Swing above the valley (RM68). A local coffee plantation follows. Lunch is at a ridgeline restaurant overlooking Mount Batur's active crater and volcanic lake. Tirta Empul sacred spring temple closes the afternoon. Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill in Kuta.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Village gold and silver workshops
Tegalalang Rice Terrace (optional Bali Swing RM68)
Lunch with Mount Batur and Lake Batur panorama
Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta

days.title: Bedugul Highlands, Floating Temple, Chocolate Factory & Tanah Lot
days.description: The cool Bedugul highlands are the morning destination. Bloom Garden showcases highland tropical flowers and hydrangeas at around 1,200 metres elevation. Pura Ulun Danu Beratan — the Floating Temple on Lake Beratan — follows, one of Bali's most photographed landmarks. Lunch at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views. Junglegold Chocolate Factory demonstrates how Bali's finest cacao is turned into artisanal chocolate. The day ends at Tanah Lot for the sunset — one of Bali's most iconic scenes. Optional one-hour Balinese massage (RM90). Dinner at Tempong Indra.
suggestedActivities: Lunch at Ulun Danu Resto with lake views

days.title: Gates of Heaven, Tirta Gangga & Kanto Lampo Waterfall
days.description: The day that sets this package apart. An early start for the drive to east Bali — Pura Lempuyang Luhur, where the famous split gate (the Gates of Heaven) frames Mount Agung in near-perfect symmetry. A reflection pool below the gate creates a mirror image that has become one of the most-photographed spots in Asia. Lunch is at Maha Gangga restaurant amid rice fields. The afternoon visits Tirta Gangga, the ornate royal water palace with koi ponds, stepping stones, and fountains set against a backdrop of rice terraces. Kanto Lampo Waterfall — a broad curtain of water over black volcanic rock — is the final stop before returning for dinner at Ayam Betutu Khas Bali.
suggestedActivities: Lempuyang Temple and Gates of Heaven photography
Lunch at Maha Gangga amid rice fields
Dinner at Ayam Betutu Khas Bali

days.title: Free Exploration Day — Private Vehicle Included
days.description: Entirely yours. A private driver and vehicle are included for up to ten hours — choose from Seminyak beach clubs, additional waterfalls, Bali Safari, Nusa Dua, temple hopping, or a spa morning. Lunch and dinner are at your own expense today.
suggestedActivities: Private vehicle and driver for up to 10 hours
Suggested options: Seminyak beach club, additional waterfalls, Bali Safari, temple hopping, spa

days.title: Leisure & Departure
days.description: A final unhurried morning — breakfast at the hotel, last souvenir shopping if needed, then a comfortable airport transfer to Ngurah Rai International Airport.
suggestedActivities: Leisure time or final souvenir shopping

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your 6-Day Bali Escape Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Six days is how long Bali deserves. Dhesu has been arranging Bali trips for Malaysian travellers since 1988 — we know which roads to take to Lempuyang before the crowds arrive, which waterfall is worth the walk, and how to sequence six days so the trip builds momentum rather than losing it.

sections.heading: 6-Day Bali Private Tour from Malaysia — The Complete Itinerary
sections.body: This six-day package is designed for Malaysian travellers who want to see all of Bali — including the sites most visitors never reach. The Gates of Heaven at Lempuyang, Tirta Gangga water palace, and Kanto Lampo Waterfall are all in east Bali, a region that most four-day packages skip entirely. Combined with the classic Ubud-Bedugul-Tanah Lot circuit and a full free day with private transport, this is the most complete Bali itinerary in the Dhesu range. Valid for daily departure until 31 March 2027.

sections.heading: Lempuyang Temple and the Gates of Heaven — What You Need to Know
sections.body: The 'Gates of Heaven' photograph requires a queue — sometimes a long one, particularly at peak times. Dhesu schedules the Lempuyang visit for the early morning to minimise waiting time. The reflection in the photograph is created by a mirror held below the camera by the temple's assistant — it is not an actual pool. This is well-known and does not diminish the experience: the temple complex itself, the views, and the sacred atmosphere are entirely genuine.

sections.heading: East Bali for Malaysian Travellers — The Forgotten Quarter
sections.body: Most Bali itineraries focus on the south (Kuta, Seminyak, Uluwatu) and the centre (Ubud, Kintamani, Bedugul). East Bali — the regency of Karangasem, with its royal water palace at Tirta Gangga, its waterfalls, and the Mother Temple of Pura Besakih on the slopes of Mount Agung — is quieter, more traditional, and in many ways more beautiful. Day four of this package gives a proper taste of east Bali at a pace that does it justice.

items.question: How early should I arrive at Lempuyang Temple for the Gates of Heaven?
items.answer: The earlier the better. Dhesu schedules the Lempuyang visit for the first activity of the day to minimise the queue. Peak visiting times are mid-morning to early afternoon. Early morning light is also better for photography.

items.question: Is Kanto Lampo Waterfall strenuous to reach?
items.answer: No. The walk to Kanto Lampo from the carpark is a short and easy path through rice fields — it takes about 10 minutes. The waterfall itself involves some rock-stepping to reach the best viewpoint, but it is not a demanding hike.

items.question: What is the best hotel tier for a 6-day stay?
items.answer: For a six-night stay, the 4-star option offers the best balance of comfort and value. The combo option (3 nights Kuta + 2 nights private villa in Canggu) is popular with travellers who want to experience both the Kuta area and Bali's more boutique Canggu neighbourhood.

items.question: Can I add a Nusa Penida day to this 6-day package?
items.answer: Yes — the free day on Day 5 is an ideal slot for a Nusa Penida fast boat trip. Contact Dhesu to add the Nusa Penida island tour to this package as a Day 5 upgrade.

items.question: Is the optional Kecak Dance on Day 1 worth doing?
items.answer: Very much so. The Kecak Fire Dance at Uluwatu is one of Southeast Asia's most spectacular cultural performances — over a hundred performers, an ocean backdrop, a sunset setting, and a Ramayana story told without instruments, only voice. At RM35, it is exceptional value and a strong recommendation for all travellers.

items.question: What is the Tirta Gangga water palace?
items.answer: Tirta Gangga was built in 1948 by Anak Agung Anglurah Ketut Karangasem, the last raja of the Karangasem kingdom, as a royal retreat and holy spring complex. It comprises multiple tiers of pools, fountains, and stepping stones set against rice field terraces, with a central spring that is still used for ritual purposes by local Balinese Hindus. Koi swim in the reflection pools and the garden is beautifully maintained.
